title: Anchorage police say moose stabbed to death in city park
summary: Anchorage police say they have three suspects in custody after a moose was stabbed to death in Russian Jack Springs Park in East Anchorage on Tuesday evening.

title: Power plant fire knocks out electricity in Emmonak
summary: When one of the four generators at the Emmonak power plant caught fire, the entire plant shut down. A fire brigade team and volunteers spent several hours containing the flames.

title: PR Services of Whitehorse, Yukon buys Skagway News
summary: Alaska Travel Publications LLP, a subsidiary of PR Services of Whitehorse, Yukon, has purchased The Skagway News, the Juneau Empire reports. The paper has been for sale since 2010.

title: Parnell and former rural adviser open consulting business
summary: Former Gov. Sean Parnell and ex-rural affairs adviser John Moller co-own a new consulting business that's looking for work and office space. In a reflective, personal commentary sent to the Alaska Republican Party, Parnell also said he's content now that the sting of his loss has passed.

title: Willow woman injured in Parks Highway rollover
summary: Three passengers, including two children, were taken to the hospital after the SUV they were riding in was destroyed in a rollover accident on the Parks Highway in Willow, Alaska State Troopers said.

title: Military plans joint training exercise in June
summary: A military training exercise in the Gulf of Alaska involving about 6,000 active duty and reserve members from all military branches and the Alaska National Guard is planned for June.

title: Troopers: 2 men with bullet wounds seek aid after shots exchanged during home invasion
summary: Three men were shot and a woman injured as home invasions were reported in two locations near Wasilla on Monday evening, authorities say. Investigators are still piecing together what happened and whether the events are linked.

title: Alaska LNG project chugging along, but uncertainties remain
summary: Alaska's proposed liquefied natural gas project is moving along, albeit slowly, as the state and its partners work to make the stars align for an LNG plant on the Kenai Peninsula and an 800-mile pipeline running from the North Slope, among other project hurdles.

title: Justice Department says Alaska, other states must improve access for Native voters
summary: The U.S. Justice Department said Monday that lagging voter turnout among Native Americans could be reversed by changing federal law to require states and localities to make it easier to vote in Alaska villages and on Indian reservations.

title: Troopers: Search for missing canoeist suspended 'indefinitely'
summary: Alaska State Troopers said a search for 33-year-old Matt Asturi, a Nevada man last seen canoeing on Upper Trail Lake last week, was suspended Monday night.

title: Shell financial reports about Arctic drilling are too 'rosy,' environmentalists charge
summary: A petition filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission seeks an investigation into Shell financial reports that critics say gloss over risks to investors from offshore Arctic drilling.

title: Pump up the jams as you head into the Alaska outdoors
summary: The most important tool you need to keep your momentum during the drive to your favorite trail head is a robust collection of wonderful tunes.

title: Icy roads lead to Parks Highway crash, Anchorage accidents
summary: A two-vehicle crash Tuesday afternoon closed both lanes of the Parks Highway near the Big Lake cutoff for more than an hour, according to troopers. And in Anchorage, roads slick from snow and freezing rain caused widespread traffic problems, police said.

title: Opponents of Prop 5, Anchorage LGBT rights initiative, accused of gay-bashing
summary: Are Jim Minnery and the Catholic Anchor bashing Anchorage's gay community in order to score votes against an initiative that enjoys broad support?

title: Bangkok tourists rescued by Troopers from Mendenhall Glacier
summary: Two Thai nationals from Bangkok, Prao Ruang and Tong Phiti, were rescued after they became lost in the dark near the West Glacier Trail's rock peninsula, Alaska State Troopers said.

title: 2015 Visitors' Guide: One-day road trips to get you out of Anchorage
summary: North or south, the highways out of Anchorage lead visitors to uniquely Alaska experiences–and get you back into town before the midnight sun dips to the horizon.

title: Wolf found dead in Denali Park with cable snare on neck
summary: The body of a 1-year-old male wolf has been found within the boundaries of Denali National Park and Preserve, prompting wolf advocates to renew calls for a ban on trapping just outside the park, the Fairbanks Daily News-Miner reports.

title: Rights Bill Sought for Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual and Transgender Americans
summary: Plans for a so-called comprehensive lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender civil rights bill are still in their infancy, and advocates say the campaign could take a decade or longer.

title: US Attacks in Afghanistan Stretch Official Parameters
summary: Months after President Obama formally declared that the long U.S.war against the Taliban was over in Afghanistan, the U.S. military is regularly conducting airstrikes against low-level insurgent forces and sending Special Operations troops directly into harm's way under the guise of "training and advising."

title: UAF water exceeds federal chemical standard but remains safe, official says
summary: The University of Alaska Fairbanks says its drinking water is safe, but recent tests showed it exceeds federal limits for chemicals formed when chlorine interacts with organic material in the water, known as "trihalomethanes," by 19 percent.

title: Salad days: Some standout greens in Anchorage and beyond
summary: With the warming weather comes the craving for things fresh and green, and that includes things that arrive on a plate. For a region with a relatively brief growing season, there are a lot of great salads on offer at restaurants in Anchorage and the surrounding areas.

title: Rhiannon Giddens goes solo with American traditions of jazz, folk and gospel
summary: Rhiannon Giddens is co-founder of the Grammy-winning Carolina Chocolate Drops and broke through as a solo artist after a star-studded folk revival concert in New York City in 2013. She'll perform in Anchorage this Sunday.

title: Reality Check: The most hard-core survival reality show isn't set in Alaska
summary: This is the most horrifying reality TV show I’ve seen in the past year, with an extremely accurate title.

title: Oil spills surprisingly common in Finland, but cleanup dependent on volunteers
summary: As vessel traffic increases, so do spill risks. The Saimaa region, home to a rare lake seal, is particularly vulnerable.

title: Nepal businesses, people out of work after quake wonder what's next
summary: As the extent of the Nepal earthquake disaster comes into focus, it's becoming clear that the temblor also shattered innumerable livelihoods in a country where many people are self-employed, insurance is a rarity and the government's social safety net is almost nonexistent.

title: Japan's Abe honors US deaths in WWII; no apology for sex slaves
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe paid homage to Americans killed in World War II on Wednesday, but he declined to apologize for his nation's role in forcing up to 200,000 women into sexual slavery during the war.

title: Don't expand Medicaid before an overhaul of its broken system
summary: OPINION: If we expand Medicaid before serious reform, we'll be making an unsustainable program even more so.

title: Discrimination is simply not the way of Christ
summary: OPINION: Amy Demboski's stand against laws protecting gays doesn't square with Christianity.

title: Demboski stuns radio host by implying opponent might approve of incest
summary: Conservative talk-show host Casey Reynolds said he thought he was throwing a softball question to mayoral candidate Amy Demboski when he asked her if she would reject assertions that her opponent, Ethan Berkowitz, would support incest marriage.

title: Bethel councilman campaigning against heroin use gets death threat
summary: A Bethel City Council member trying to draw attention to the destructiveness of heroin in the Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta says he received a death threat related to his advocacy and police are investigating.

title: Bernie Sanders to challenge Hillary Clinton
summary: Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders is preparing to enter the race for the Democratic presidential nomination on Thursday, according to a person familiar with Sanders' plans.

title: Arts Scene: Bookstore day, baseball and portrait photography
summary: Saturday, May 2, will be Independent Bookstore Day, and Palmer’s exemplary independent bookstore, Fireside Books, will host Heather Lende from Haines, author of “Find the Good;” “Take Good Care of the Garden and the Dogs” and “If You Lived Here, I’d Know Your Name.”

title: Arctic Council ministerial -- winners and losers
summary: With the U.S. taking leadership of the Arctic Council, climate change moves to the forefront of the agenda and some Canadian priorities take a back seat.

title: Anchorage student Maeva Ordaz wins national poetry competition
summary: Maeva Ordaz, a senior at West High School in Anchorage, has won the 2015 Poetry Out Loud national competition and a prize of $20,000.

title: Anchorage schools, state split on whether April bond will be reimbursed
summary: The Walker administration says assertions by legislators and others that the April 7 school bond measure in Anchorage would be excluded from the new school bond reimbursement moratorium were incorrect.

title: Alaska politicians should stop pointing fingers and use them to plug the budget leak
summary: OPINION: Unfortunately, political labels seem to be more important in the Capitol building than results. This is a big part of the reason we have ended up in the fiscal mess we are in.

title: Alaska cities receive mixed grades on 'State of the Air' report
summary: For ozone pollution, Fairbanks is the cleanest metropolitan area in the country, but it's also the ninth worst for short-term particle pollution, according to the American Lung Association.

title: 4 of 5 Government Hill bears captured by Fish and Game
summary: Biologists have now captured four of the five black bears roaming Anchorage's Government Hill neighborhood, including the mother. One cub remains at large.

title: Watch while gray whales chow down in Resurrection Bay this spring
summary: When the first gray whale breached, it caught many of us off guard. My 10-year-old pointed toward the stern, mouth hanging open, as again and again the gray whale presented us with an impressive display of marine mammal athleticism. The atmosphere was electric after that.

title: State vows to appeal after Alaska's same-sex marriage ban struck down
summary: A U.S. District Court judge on Sunday struck down Alaska's ban on same-sex marriage as unconstitutional, but the state quickly said it will appeal the decision.

title: Alan Boraas: Smarter nets may help us steward Alaska's mighty salmon runs
summary: King salmon are not yet decimated in the Kenai and Kasilof rivers, but they are at the dangerously low end of modified Riker model escapement estimates necessary to sustain the species.

title: Readers write: Letters to the editor, April 27, 2015
summary: LETTERS: Readers sound off on issues close to the bone.

title: Anchorage Assembly to consider alcohol purchase restrictions for some sleep-off center users
summary: Mayor Dan Sullivan’s administration is proposing to restrict alcohol purchases for people who use Anchorage’s emergency sleep-off center three or more times in one year.

title: Alaskan among four killed in Washington helicopter crash
summary: The four Army aviators killed Monday when two helicopters crashed during training at a Washington state base included a helicopter pilot who grew up in Alaska, Chief Warrant Officer Shan Joseph Satterfield.

title: Bethel's Klejka wins Junior Iditarod
summary: A 17-year-old from Bethel captured the Junior Iditarod championship Sunday in his third running of the mid-distance sled dog race for teens.

title: 4 of 5 Government Hill bears captured by Fish and Game
summary: Biologists have now captured four of the five black bears roaming Anchorage's Government Hill neighborhood, including the mother. One cub remains at large.

title: Alan Boraas: Zimmerman, Martin came together in a clash of values
summary: Repeatedly we are told that Trayvon Martin's killing was not about race and racial profiling; neither, we are told, was it about stand your ground and guns. Perhaps, but it was about something, and the most likely context to understand the tragedy is as a confrontation of core values.

title: Map reveals movements of urban Anchorage bears
summary: The Alaska Department of Fish and Game has released an online map detailing the lives of nine bears living near and in Anchorage, from Turnagain Arm to Fire Island.

title: Tanana man dies in ATV crash
summary: Alaska State Troopers say a 46-year-old man from Tanana died Sunday after he lost control of his ATV and it crashed into a tree.
